news.headline: PIMCO predicts 30-35% chance of US recession in 2025, while dismissing concern of stagflation. Economy resilient with strong private sector balance sheets.
news.summary: Amid trade tensions and the Federal Reserve's economic outlook, PIMCO has forecasted a 30-35% probability of a recession, ruling out the chance of stagflation in the U.S. in 2025. Economist Tiffany Wilding from PIMCO mentioned that despite uncertainties, the U.S. economy remains robust with strong private sector balance sheets. Wilding addressed the Deutsche Bank survey indicating nearly 50% odds of a recession, countering that historical data suggests a 15-20% probability annually, and current conditions might elevate it to around 30-35%. While acknowledging increased risks, she emphasized the resilience of the economy.

news.headline: US stocks likely to open lower after 3-day advance, reacting to Trump's mixed tariff signals. Futures trading lower.Key numbers:- 10-year Treasury yield: 4.33%- Two-year yield: 4.02%- CME Group FedWatch: 88.4% chance of unchanged interest rates in May meetingFutures:- Nasdaq 100: -0.15%- S&P 500: -0.12%- Dow Jones: -0.16%- Russell 2000: -0.21%SPY and QQQ ETFs are down in premarket trading.Consumer discretionary, communication services, financials, and energy sectors led market rally on Tuesday. Home prices hit 20th record high. Overall, market appears more balanced.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures were trading lower on Wednesday following three consecutive sessions of gains. Investors reacted cautiously to mixed tariff signals from President Trump, causing volatility in the market. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.33%, and the two-year yield stood at 4.02%. There is an 88.4% chance of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in its May meeting, as per CME Group's FedWatch tool. Futures for major benchmark indices were down: Nasdaq 100 by 0.15%, S&P 500 by 0.12%, Dow Jones by 0.16%, and Russell 2000 by 0.21%. ETFs tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 (SPY and QQQ) also declined in premarket. In the last session, various sectors led a market rally, but home prices hit a record high, and consumer confidence declined. The Dow Jones rose by 4 points to 42,587.50, and the S&P 500 was up by 0.16% at 5,776.65 on Tuesday.

news.headline: CFOs' concerns over tariffs and economic uncertainty rise in Q1 2025: 15% alarmed by duties, optimism dips to 62.1.
news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, there was a significant decline in economic optimism among chief financial officers, according to the latest CFO Survey by Duke University, and the Federal Reserve Banks of Richmond and Atlanta. The survey revealed that economic optimism among CFOs dropped to 62.1 from 66.0 in the previous quarter, undoing much of the post-election increase. Concerns about trade policy and tariffs saw a notable increase, impacting almost 1/3 of the surveyed firms, a substantial rise from the previous quarter. Issues around market uncertainty also emerged as a top-five concern. The survey provides insights from financial decision makers and highlights that while optimism about individual firm finances decreased, it was not as significant as the overall economic sentiment decline. For more details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

Consumer inflation expectations have spiked, making it harder for the Fed to cut interest rates. University of Michigan reports inflation expectations at 4.9% in March and long-term expectations at 3.9%. Rising concerns over structurally persistent inflation.
news.summary: Consumer inflation expectations have surged, with the University of Michigan's Survey of Consumers showing a rise to 4.9% in March from 4.3% in February for year-ahead inflation forecasts. Longer-term expectations spiked to 3.9%, the highest since 1993. These numbers indicate a potentially more persistent inflation trend, impacting the Federal Reserve's ability to reduce interest rates further. Goldman Sachs' Elsie Peng emphasized caution in response to this data, as elevated inflation expectations can lead to self-fulfilling prophecies. This spike in inflation expectations adds uncertainty to the possibility of interest rate cuts this year. CFOs are increasingly worried about tariffs as well.

Advance Auto Parts aims to open 130+ new stores by 2027 after closure phase completion. Over 75% of current stores hold top positions in key markets. Company plans to open 30 new U.S. locations in 2025 and at least 100 more through 2027.
news.summary: Advance Auto Parts Inc. (NYSE:AAP) has successfully concluded the store closure phase of its transformation strategy and is now focusing on returning to growth. Currently, more than 75% of the company's stores are located in markets where it holds the top two positions in terms of store density, consolidating its presence in crucial areas. Moving forward, the company aims to open 30 new U.S. locations by 2025 and a minimum of 100 additional stores by 2027, including larger "market hubs." This strategic shift marks a significant step for Advance Auto Parts Inc. as it progresses towards expanding its footprint in the retail sector.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that global carbon dioxide levels have reached a record high of 416 parts per million (ppm) in 2019. This increase is mainly attributed to human activities, particularly the burning of fossil fuels. Scientists warn that this surge in CO2 levels is accelerating climate change and its detrimental effects on the planet. The data also shows that the rate of increase has been consistent over the past decade, with an average rise of 2.5 ppm per year. This concerning trend highlights the urgent need for nations to take decisive actions to mitigate greenhouse gas emissions and curb global warming.

news.headline: Avalanche, Gelato team up for enterprise chains. New service streamlines deployment, lowers costs. Avalanche TVL over $1.1B. Crypto industry ready for institutional adoption crucial. Prominent brands adopt Gelato’s platform.
news.summary: Gelato, a popular blockchain developer platform, is launching a new blockchain-as-a-service solution on Avalanche to cater to the rising demand for sovereign blockchain infrastructure amid institutional adoption. This upgrade will assist developers in deploying fully sovereign chains faster and cheaper with full interoperability via Avalanche InterChain Messaging (ICM). Gelato's services are ideal for advanced applications such as fintech and specialized gaming economies, offering companies the ability to quickly deploy independent blockchains with reduced costs and quicker launch times. Avalanche, the 10th largest blockchain network in the industry, holds over $1.1 billion in total value locked (TVL) across its DeFi applications. The launch of this new solution aligns with the growing need for reliable infrastructure in order to fuel institutional crypto adoption. Gelato's founder, Luis Schliesske, highlighted the importance of robust infrastructure for institutions to have confidence in adopting blockchain technology and crypto offerings. Notable brands like Fox News and eBay have already utilized Gelato's development platform for blockchain-based solutions, showcasing its credibility in the industry.

news.headline: ADM reported 22.4 million passengers in 2024 at YUL Airport, up 5.8% from 2023. EBITDA was $439.1 million, revenue $917.2 million, and net income $182.0 million. Operating expenses were $331.3 million, with transfers to govt of $146.8 million. Net debt was $2.21 billion.
news.summary: In 2024, ADM Aéroports de Montréal reported positive results and growth in key areas. YUL Montréal-Trudeau International Airport saw 5.1 million passengers in the fourth quarter, up 2.4% from 2023, and 22.4 million passengers in total, a 5.8% increase. EBITDA for 2024 was $439.1 million, up $1.2 million from the previous year. Capital investments were $456.5 million, up 39.6% from 2023. Consolidated revenues reached $917.2 million, an 8.2% increase. Net income was $182.0 million, up 36.9% from 2023. ADM's net debt as of December 31, 2024, was $2.21 billion. The company is focused on enhancing passenger experience with the Flight Plan 2028 initiative, starting construction this summer.
